title: "there's no SMHConfig in my xorg"
date: 2007-03-01
forum: Absolute Beginner Talk
---

### Post by pilot550 on 2007-03-01
I am trying to the side scroll on my touch pad to work. I installed GSynaptics with high hopes. When I try and run it I get this error message:

GSynaptics couldn't initialize.
You have to set 'SHMConfig' 'true' in xorg.conf or XF86Config to use GSynaptics

I tried to do what it said but SMHConfig isn't in my xorg.conf file. Here's what I got:

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"

	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"

	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"

	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"

	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"

  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Corporation Mobile 915GM/GMS/910GML Express Graphics Controller"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
EndSection

	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"

	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"

EndSection

	Mode	0666
EndSection


Little help please.

---

### Post by mcduck on 2007-03-02
You need to add it there yourself, in the Input section for Synaptics.

Run 'gksudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf' to open the file for editing in Gedit. (or 'sudo nano /etc/X11/xorg.conf' to use a command-line editor)

---

### Post by Jussi01 on 2007-03-02
change this:

```
Section "InputDevice"
Identifier "Synaptics Touchpad"
Driver "synaptics"
Option "SendCoreEvents" "true"
Option "Device" "/dev/psaux"
Option "Protocol" "auto-dev"
Option "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection
```

to this

```
Section "InputDevice"
Identifier "Synaptics Touchpad"
Driver "synaptics"
Option "SendCoreEvents" "true"
Option "Device" "/dev/psaux"
Option "Protocol" "auto-dev"
Option "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
Option "SHMConfig" "true"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by doutdes on 2007-07-22
thnx and indeed it was not only missing the setting you posted but the whole synaptics input.

     I checked the xconf.org file and it was empty like the nvidia settings make one from scratch, so I had to run the dexconf command and the it recreated the xconf, org, after that run the nvidia again to merge the setting with the new xorg.conf file and then Ctrl+Alt+Backspace to reload the Xorg

---
